title: Evaluation — Cross-Validation with Full MITBIH Dataset
summary: Used the full MIT-BIH Atrial Fibrillation Database as an independent cross-validation set to evaluate models trained on LTAFDB. Compared multiple model architectures.
completed_tasks: Applied full preprocessing pipeline to all 22 MITBIH records (bandpass filter, resample 250→128 Hz, normalize, 30s windows)
Used MITBIH dataset as independent cross-validation set (no overlap with LTAFDB training data)
Trained and evaluated models: XGBoost, Random Forest, CNN, LSTM, CNN+LSTM hybrid
Computed evaluation metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC
Analyzed model performance across both LTAFDB test split and MITBIH cross-validation set
Identified best-performing model architecture for deployment

next_week_plan.focus: Deployment — build and launch Streamlit application
goals: Build interactive Streamlit app for real-time AFib detection
Integrate best-performing model from Week 7 evaluation
Implement live ECG signal input and preprocessing pipeline in-app
Display AFib probability output and SHAP-based explainability visualization
Deploy app publicly via Streamlit Community Cloud
